title: Ordonanța de urgență a Guvernului nr. 98/2010 privind identificarea, desemnarea și protecția infrastructurilor critice
title_en: Government Emergency Ordinance 98/2010 on the identification, designation and protection of critical infrastructure
description: Romania's transposition of Directive 2008/114/EC on European Critical Infrastructure (ECI), establishing the framework for identifying, designating and protecting national and European critical infrastructures.

provisions.title: Scope
provisions.content: This emergency ordinance establishes the procedure for identifying, designating and protecting critical infrastructures, transposing Directive 2008/114/EC of the Council of 8 December 2008 on the identification and designation of European critical infrastructures and the assessment of the need to improve their protection. This ordinance applies to national critical infrastructure (ICN) and European critical infrastructure (ICE) located on the territory of Romania.

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this ordinance: a) critical infrastructure means an element, system, or part thereof located on the territory of Romania which is essential for the maintenance of vital societal functions, health, safety, security, economic or social well-being of the population, and the disruption or destruction of which would have a significant impact on Romania; b) European critical infrastructure (ICE) means critical infrastructure located in Romania the disruption or destruction of which would have a significant impact on at least two Member States; c) operator of critical infrastructure means the natural or legal person responsible for the operation and management of a critical infrastructure; d) operator security plan (PSO) means a document identifying the critical infrastructure assets and the security measures implemented for their protection.

provisions.title: Critical Infrastructure Sectors
provisions.content: Critical infrastructures shall be identified in the following sectors: a) energy (electricity, oil, gas); b) transport (air, rail, water, road); c) information and communication technology; d) water supply; e) food supply; f) health; g) financial services; h) public administration; i) public order and national security; j) industry (chemical, nuclear); k) space. The Government may extend the list of sectors by decision.

provisions.title: Identification Criteria
provisions.content: The identification of critical infrastructure shall be based on cross-cutting criteria, including: a) the casualty criterion - assessed in terms of the potential number of fatalities or injuries; b) the economic effects criterion - assessed in terms of the significance of economic loss or degradation of products or services; c) the public effects criterion - assessed in terms of the impact on public confidence, physical suffering and disruption of daily life. Sectoral criteria specific to each sector shall also be applied.

provisions.title: Designation Process
provisions.content: The designation of national critical infrastructure shall be carried out by the competent sectoral authorities in coordination with the Centre for Coordination of Critical Infrastructure Protection (CPCIC). European critical infrastructure designation shall be carried out in consultation with the Member States concerned, in accordance with the bilateral and multilateral procedures established by Directive 2008/114/EC. The designation shall be reviewed at least every two years.

provisions.title: CPCIC Functions
provisions.content: The Centre for Coordination of Critical Infrastructure Protection (CPCIC) shall: a) coordinate the overall process of critical infrastructure identification and designation at the national level; b) serve as the contact point for European critical infrastructure matters with the European Commission and other Member States; c) manage the national database of critical infrastructures; d) provide guidance and support to operators of critical infrastructure in developing operator security plans; e) coordinate the national risk assessment relating to critical infrastructure.

provisions.title: Operator Security Plans
provisions.content: Each operator of a designated critical infrastructure shall develop and maintain an Operator Security Plan (PSO - Plan de Securitate al Operatorului). The PSO shall include: a) identification of important assets; b) a risk analysis based on major threat scenarios, vulnerability assessment, and potential impact analysis; c) identification, selection and prioritisation of countermeasures and procedures, distinguishing between permanent and graduated security measures; d) emergency and recovery procedures. The PSO shall be reviewed and updated at least annually.

provisions.title: Security Liaison Officer
provisions.content: Each operator of a designated European critical infrastructure shall designate a Security Liaison Officer (OLS - Ofițer de Legătură pentru Securitate), in accordance with Article 6 of Directive 2008/114/EC. The Security Liaison Officer shall act as the point of contact for security-related issues between the operator and the competent sectoral authority and the CPCIC. The OLS shall coordinate the implementation of the operator security plan and liaise with national and EU authorities.

provisions.title: Risk Assessment
provisions.content: The CPCIC, in coordination with the competent sectoral authorities, shall conduct a national risk assessment relating to critical infrastructure at least every three years. The risk assessment shall consider: a) all-hazards approach covering natural disasters, technological accidents, and intentional attacks including terrorism and cyber threats; b) interdependencies and cascading effects between different critical infrastructure sectors; c) cross-border risks and dependencies on critical infrastructure in other Member States.

provisions.title: Physical Security Measures
provisions.content: Operators of designated critical infrastructure shall implement appropriate physical security measures, including: a) perimeter protection and access control systems; b) detection and surveillance systems; c) protection of key assets and systems; d) personnel security measures, including background checks for personnel with access to sensitive areas; e) resilience measures ensuring the continuity of essential functions during disruptions.

provisions.title: Cyber Security Measures
provisions.content: Operators of designated critical infrastructure whose operations depend on network and information systems shall implement appropriate cyber security measures in coordination with the requirements of Law 362/2018 on cybersecurity. These measures shall include: a) network segmentation and access control for operational technology (OT) systems; b) monitoring and detection of cyber incidents; c) incident response and recovery procedures; d) regular security testing and vulnerability assessments; e) supply chain security for digital components.

provisions.chapter: Chapter IV - Information Sharing and Cooperation
provisions.title: Information Sharing
provisions.content: Information relating to the identification, designation and protection of critical infrastructure shall be classified as service secrets or state secrets, as appropriate, in accordance with the applicable legislation on the protection of classified information. The exchange of information on European critical infrastructure between Romania and other Member States and the European Commission shall be carried out in accordance with the security arrangements established under Directive 2008/114/EC.

provisions.chapter: Chapter IV - Information Sharing and Cooperation
provisions.title: International Cooperation
provisions.content: Romania shall cooperate with other Member States and with the European Commission for the purpose of improving the protection of European critical infrastructure, in accordance with Article 7 of Directive 2008/114/EC. The CPCIC shall participate in the contact points meetings and shall contribute to the development of common guidelines and best practices for the protection of critical infrastructure.

provisions.title: Supervision
provisions.content: The competent sectoral authorities, in coordination with the CPCIC, shall supervise compliance with the requirements of this ordinance by operators of critical infrastructure. Supervisory activities shall include inspections, audits, and the review of operator security plans. The CPCIC shall report to the Government annually on the status of critical infrastructure protection in Romania.

provisions.title: Sanctions
provisions.content: Non-compliance with the requirements of this ordinance shall be sanctioned with administrative fines. Failure to develop or update the operator security plan shall be sanctioned with a fine from 10,000 to 50,000 RON. Failure to designate a security liaison officer shall be sanctioned with a fine from 5,000 to 25,000 RON. Failure to notify relevant incidents to the competent authorities shall be sanctioned with a fine from 5,000 to 30,000 RON. Repeated non-compliance may result in the revocation of operating licences.

provisions.title: Emergency Measures
provisions.content: In situations of imminent threat to critical infrastructure, the Government, on the proposal of the CPCIC and the competent sectoral authorities, may order emergency protection measures, including the deployment of additional security forces, the restriction of access to the critical infrastructure and its vicinity, and the activation of emergency response plans. These measures shall be proportionate and limited in duration.

provisions.title: Implementing Regulations
provisions.content: The Government shall adopt implementing regulations within 90 days from the entry into force of this ordinance, establishing detailed criteria for the identification and designation of critical infrastructure, the content and format of operator security plans, and the procedures for information exchange with other Member States and the European Commission.

provisions.title: Relationship with CER Directive
provisions.content: This ordinance shall be applied in conjunction with any national legislation transposing Directive (EU) 2022/2557 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the resilience of critical entities (CER Directive), which will replace Directive 2008/114/EC. Until the transposition of the CER Directive into national law, this ordinance shall continue to apply.

provisions.title: Transitional Provisions
provisions.content: Operators of existing critical infrastructure shall submit their operator security plans within 6 months from the date of designation. Critical infrastructure designated before the entry into force of this ordinance shall be subject to review under the criteria established herein within 12 months from the entry into force.

provisions.title: Entry into Force
provisions.content: This emergency ordinance shall enter into force on the date of its publication in Monitorul Oficial al României. This ordinance transposes Directive 2008/114/EC of the Council of 8 December 2008 on the identification and designation of European critical infrastructures and the assessment of the need to improve their protection.

definitions.definition: An element, system, or part thereof located on the territory of Romania which is essential for the maintenance of vital societal functions, health, safety, security, economic or social well-being of the population, and the disruption or destruction of which would have a significant impact.
Critical infrastructure located in Romania the disruption or destruction of which would have a significant impact on at least two Member States.
A document identifying the critical infrastructure assets and the security measures implemented for their protection, including risk analysis, countermeasures, and emergency procedures.
Centre for Coordination of Critical Infrastructure Protection - the national coordination body for critical infrastructure protection in Romania.
